summary: Criteria a factory cell run should satisfy to count as 'good' work in this repo. Concrete-first: this rubric is calibrated against the ai-stack-local-smoke factory and the workflow GPT Pro reviewed across PRs #1-#3.

criteria.title: Wraps the actual lane, not a proxy
criteria.rationale: A factory must run the real script that launchd schedules, not just an inner tool. PR #1 v1 was rejected for wrapping `frontier mcp smoke --read-only` while claiming to wrap `ai-stack.local-smoke-nightly`. The primary verifier is the lane script; inner checks are supplementary.

criteria.title: Final classification is a single source of truth
criteria.rationale: Pass / fail / ambiguous must be derived in one place from primary status + repair status + kill switch + escalations. Side effects (alerts, ledger payloads, exit codes) read from the final classification, never from the raw primary. PR #1 v1 leaked a false-green when stale repair coexisted with primary=ok.

criteria.title: Classification is mutually exclusive and total
criteria.rationale: Every outcome lands in exactly one of {passed, failed, ambiguous}. The space is enumerated and tested. No outcome can be both passed and ambiguous; no outcome is unclassified.

criteria.title: passed implies invariants
criteria.rationale: classification == 'passed' MUST imply: kill switch inactive, primary ok, repair ok, escalations empty. Asserted by an enumeration test over the full state space. The 'no false green' guarantee is the load-bearing property of the factory.

criteria.title: Bounded repair is non-destructive by default
criteria.rationale: Repairs verify state, they don't auto-fix. Stale config produces an escalation, not a silent edit. If a repair MUST be destructive, it requires explicit policy + rollback + approval; this is documented in the spec, not implicit.

criteria.title: Kill switch is observable and load-bearing
criteria.rationale: The kill-switch path is in the spec, the active flag is surfaced in any context, and the short-circuit happens before any verifier / inner / repair / ledger / alert side effect. A flipped kill switch terminates the run with classification=ambiguous and zero side effects.

criteria.title: Run-ledger entries are written for non-killed runs
criteria.rationale: Every active run opens a session and writes (system: factory.run_start) → (ops.repair_start) → (ops.repair_end with classification + escalations + repair status) → optional (alert) → (system: factory.run_end). This is the audit trail; nothing else writes to the ledger from the factory.

criteria.title: Alert severity reflects FINAL classification
criteria.rationale: Mapping is in the spec under alert.severityByFinalClassification. passed → no alert. failed → high. ambiguous → medium. Notify-alerts.sh picks these up directly via the existing ledger query — no new alert plumbing needed.

criteria.title: Evidence is captured before any repair attempt
criteria.rationale: Failure logs, alert IDs, git state, smoke output get committed to evidence/ at the start of work. The bounded repair runs against this captured state. Repairs without prior evidence are blind.

criteria.title: External integration surfaces are read-only from the factory
criteria.rationale: The factory does not edit /Users/test/bin scripts, launchd plists, or other repo cells. It reads them via spec, treats them as documented surfaces, and escalates if they're stale. Any modification to those surfaces is a separate, explicit operation outside the factory.

non_goals: Generic FactoryRunner abstraction — concrete-first; do not generalize until a second factory exists.
Self-grading: the factory's own tests are written by the same agent that wrote the implementation; independent eval suites + reviews are how this rubric gets enforced, not unit tests alone.
Performance benchmarking — factory cells are observability + bounded repair, not throughput-critical.
Multi-tenancy / multi-lane support in a single cell.
CI / scheduling concerns — those belong to launchd or to a higher orchestration layer.

calibration.exemplar: factories/ai-stack-local-smoke/ — passes 15/15 criteria in evals/factory-quality/run.ts as of HEAD
